name: White Chocolate Ravioli
recipeIngredient: 3 oz White chocolate bars (e.g., Tobler), halved crosswise
1/2 cup Hazelnuts, toasted and husked - coarsely chopped
8 oz Bittersweet or semisweet Chocolate, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the chocolate and butter in a double boiler over barely simmering water. Stir until smooth.
2. Transfer to a medium bowl. Whisk egg yolks in another bowl.
3. Beat egg whites, salt, and cream of tartar in a separate bowl until soft peaks form.
4. Add 2 tablespoons sugar and beat until stiff but not dry. Fold into chocolate mixture.
5. In another bowl, beat whipping cream with 2 tablespoons sugar and vanilla until slightly thickened.
6. Fold the whipped cream into the chocolate mixture.
7. Pour into a metal bowl. Cover and chill overnight.
8. Chill ravioli mold in freezer.
9. Preheat oven to its lowest setting for 5 minutes, then turn off the oven.
10. Place white chocolate on baking sheets and place them in the turned-off oven. Let stand until soft enough to yield when pressed with a finger (about 5 minutes).
11. Roll one piece of chocolate out on parchment paper to flatten slightly. Lift chocolate from paper using a thin knife if necessary.
12. Turn pasta machine to the widest setting. Run chocolate through, adjusting to the next narrower setting after each run until it is about 1/16 inch thick.
13. Quickly press chocolate into chilled ravioli mold.
14. Fill each ravioli with 1 to 1 1/2 tablespoons of mousse filling. Repeat rolling with the second piece of chocolate, resoftening in the oven as necessary. Complete and seal ravioli with a rolling pin.
15. Invert mold, gently pressing to release ravioli. Cut into separate pieces if needed using a ravioli cutter or knife.
16. Place on chilled baking sheet and refrigerate.
Before serving, let chocolate ravioli stand at room temperature for 30 minutes. Arrange four raviolis on each plate. Sprinkle with nuts and garnish with mint.
